recentTweets.text: I went down to the Kerem Shalom crossing between Israel and Gaza to meet Palestinian families evacuating the enclave so their children could get medical treatment. These are their stories. https://t.co/Ffu2cRIu5f
Israelis took to the streets in large numbers on Sunday for countrywide demonstrations and a strike, calling to end the war in Gaza and return the hostages. My latest https://t.co/3JYER0lCyD

The Journal had a rare opportunity to view an aid site that is part of a controversial Israeli-backed aid plan designed to squeeze out the UN and Hamas. The distribution effort we saw was chaotic with the site overrun and reveals major problems. A 🧵https://t.co/P4Ia8kqHDl

Left-wing Jewish activists in purple vests from Standing Together, an Arab-Israeli coexistence movement, protect Palestinians who are attacked by settlers during Jerusalem Day. Activists from Standing Together hold up photos of slain Palestinian children at weekly protests
I wrote about this trend for @WSJ https://t.co/iLAcPoj7H3

The Israeli public and media are talking about the killing of the al-Najjar family children in Gaza in a way I haven’t seen before since the start of the war. The story has topped major news sites and reflects the way Israelis are increasingly turning against the war. https://t.co/5z0seFwAaT

Israeli settlers took to online chat groups to follow the movements of released prisoners and plot their attacks on Palestinian towns in the West Bank to where they were being returned as part of the ceasefire. My latest with @omarabdelb for @WSJ https://t.co/iDN6hNH5rF
Reports that the Israeli military withdrew from the Yarmouk Basin in Syria, an area that was resisting Israeli military presence and efforts to disarm the population

Israel is finding troves of Russian weapons in Hezbollah’s hands including Kornets from 2020 in the latest sign Russia is arming Israel’s foes. Israel is still careful to not anger Russia and has avoided militarily supporting 🇺🇦 My latest https://t.co/5hvDn7mZjq

I joined @AmanpourCoPBS yesterday to talk about my recent article on how the slain observers at Nahal Oz base became a symbol for Israel’s intelligence failure on Oct. 7. You can watch the full interview on Youtube here 
https://t.co/hot7COISNu

Makeshift memorial for the 7 victims of the terror attack in Jaffa killed while waiting for the train last week. Both Jews and Arabs live in the mixed city of Jaffa and the handwritten posters in Hebrew call for coexistence. https://t.co/yzKwZPjO8C

For months before Oct. 7, Israeli observers at Nahal Oz warned that Hamas was planning an attack. The government and military’s failure to listen  and protect them has made them the symbol for the Oct. 7 intelligence and operational failure. My latest https://t.co/ercg7mOwpA
Protests in Tel Aviv tonight calling to accept Biden’s deal, bring back the hostages, and stop the war https://t.co/H7Xs0Q5w1o
Excited to announce that I’m joining staff at @WSJ as News Assistant in Tel Aviv. You can send me tips and story ideas at anat.peled@wsj.com

After two Israeli mothers were told that their kidnapped sons were murdered by Hamas they set out on a journey to discover the truth. They discovered a grim reality: some of the hostages in Gaza have been killed in Israel’s military offensive there against Hamas. https://t.co/FdTLus5Z5q

Israel is collecting evidence of crimes including murder, sexual violence and torture committed by Hamas on Oct 7 for one of the the most important trials in its history. My latest with @RoryWSJ takes a look at some of this evidence. https://t.co/zvoH5FXYS1

Saar Idan, who attended the late-night protest, carried a sign with a photo of his abducted cousin, Tsachi Idan, 49. After the news of the mistaken shooting, he was very worried that his cousin had been killed. “These have been hard days with the announcements of dead hostages.” https://t.co/ENaiuDo3az

Kibbutz Be’eri is one of the hardest-hit communities of October 7 with over 90 member killed and many taken hostage. The sign outside the dead sea hotel where they were evacuated to reads: “We lost enough. We must bring the hostages home now.” https://t.co/CEJmw2nPhK

“Sometimes you have no choice,” says Mandleblit, Baharav-Miara’s predecessor, arguing that the judicial overhaul is undermining democracy. “You have to stand against that and protect the system of government. That is your job.” New for @WSJ  w/ @DovLieber https://t.co/Oaqbc5vUsY

“Today, I come to work to ensure no one does more damage,” says a civil servant who served for 10 years.” In my latest for @haaretzcom I spoke to civil  servants who describe a state of chaos under the current government. https://t.co/dkvm5HAdEt

When @DovMorell was young he was a radical hilltop youth convicted of incitement to violence and terrorism. Today he is a liberal Meretz party supporter watching his childhood settler friends calling the shots from the Knesset. My latest for @haaretzcom https://t.co/6Q5moUiYCq

Hundreds of abandoned cars on highway 1 leading to Jerusalem. They belong to tens of thousands of protesters who are marching in a last-ditch effort to stop the Netanyahu government’s judicial overhaul. https://t.co/pbdv6t34lH

“Israel will go the same way if people don’t stop this.” I sat down with Ugandan poet @AnnahAshaba to discuss her activism, the importance of asking the ‘wrong questions,’ and the similarities she sees between Uganda and Israel. New for @haaretzcom https://t.co/fmZEYmJXSe

From detention by Hezbollah in Lebanon to traveling to lran under a different name. I spoke to some of the Israeli researchers who risk travel to enemy states to bring to the world the perspectives of the people on the ground. My latest for @haaretzcom https://t.co/rI83pWAIyh
The religious right has been distorting Israel’s founding story of Zionism for many year by erasing its secular and socialist roots. My latest for @haaretzcom https://t.co/nIM9I07xzq

למה לא מדברים בישראל על 16 אזרחים ישראלים שנהרגו בלחימה באוקראינה? ומה זה אומר על החברה שלנו? גאה שהכתבה הזאת יצאה ב4 שפות: אנגלית, אוקראינית, רוסית, ועכשיו סוף סוף בשפה שהייתה הכי חשובה לי - עברית. https://t.co/gahQvczH4M
You can read the full article here (message for PDF) https://t.co/B27YVVD5jY
In today’s Haaretz paper. Glad that the story was also picked up by two Israeli-Russian media outlets @DetalyNews and @9tv_israel https://t.co/GrjDONhuPv
Why aren’t the stories of Israelis dying fighting in Ukraine being covered in Israel? My latest for @haaretzcom https://t.co/B27YVVD5jY
What can Israelis learn from the successful movement that helped  bring down Serbia’s dictator? My latest for @haaretzcom with @ivanmarovic https://t.co/48J4M43HD7

It’s hard to assess historic events as they are unfolding but I have a feeling that tonight will be remembered as the the night things changed in Israel. Something has snapped. https://t.co/N2I7DovAxC

After Netanyahu fires the defense minister for speaking out against the judicial coup tens of thousands spontaneously protest across the country Israeli flags in hand. Unbelievable scenes in Tel Aviv tonight. https://t.co/SKmwqa42CS

Passover themed protest sign in Tel Aviv tonight: “the torah speaks of 4 sons:  the wicked one (Bibi), the convicted one (Arye Dery), the cruel one (Yariv Levin) and the one who can’t speak English (Bezalel Smotrich) https://t.co/jR1Q0ya2lq
Being in Israel these days I find myself alternating between extremes: utter despair from the coalition’s actions and uplifting inspiration from the historic civic awakening taking place here.

My latest for @haaretzcom on the surprising anti-government protests in Ashdod. Saturday marked an escalation in violence against peaceful protesters as the country nears a boiling point. https://t.co/zlX566TrQ4
10th consecutive week of pro-democracy protests in Israel. Spotted in Tel Aviv: “We won’t break” https://t.co/4II1MOgvGk

Powerful protest and strike today in Israel against Netanyahu’s judicial overhaul. Well over 100k protested in front of the Israeli Parliament as the so called “reform” of the judicial branch began to be voted on https://t.co/8LsXnFiPwS
From tonight’s pro-democracy anti-Netanyahu protest in Tel Aviv: “My grandfather and grandmother didn’t come from Morocco for another king” https://t.co/svTDdTPObK
An estimated 100,000 took to the streets tonight to protest the new Netanyahu government’s destruction of Israeli democracy. https://t.co/lof1PkGNSX
My latest for @haaretzcom: the threat to Israel has changed form so the reponse needs to change too. Why street protests alone won’t cut it this time around. https://t.co/4vp6sOgRAK
My piece on the first-time vote of immigrants from Russia & Ukraine who arrived in Israel following the war in Haaretz’s print edition today. https://t.co/KsXqBGVxff
My piece on the Israeli elections for @Grand_Continent now in Spanish 👇

My latest - a primer on Israel’s election next week for @Grand_Continent. Why is Israel going to elections once again and what is at stake? Excited to be published in French for the first time. https://t.co/RASF5tzik1
The most dangerous and important development of the upcoming Israeli elections is the rise of this pyromaniac and his growing popularity with young people.

כתבה חשובה של @magazineLiberal על הילדים האוקראינים שהגיעו לישראל בעקבות המלחמה. כל הזמן אומרים לנו שהם עולים חדשים ושהם נוחתים כאן נותנים להם דגלי ישראל. אבל האמת היא שהם פליטים והם בטראומה. רבים הגיעו בלי האבות והאחים שלהם שנשארו באוקראינה להילחם. https://t.co/MVxK7WUwyH

היום בגיל 27, הייתי בסיור המודרך הראשון שלי במזרח ירושלים דרך @YozmatGeneva.  מדריך מעולה, סיור מבוסס עובדות במקום אמוציה, וחשיפה למציאות העגומה בשטח. הלוואי שהייתי עוברת סיור כזה בגיל יותר צעיר. https://t.co/FFnTwZUUHZ
שנה טובה מהאקרופוליס באתונה! https://t.co/ukXxtS4nVV

אתמול התחלתי לדבר עם מישהי ערבייה בערבית. בתגובה היא ענתה: ״מוזר לי לדבר עברית עם יהודים. זה מרגיש לי כמו חקירה בשב״כ״. הייתי טיפה מופתעת מהתגובה אבל וואלה היא צודקת. יותר מדי יהודים בישראל לומדים ערבית או ״ביטחונית״ רק מהצבא. מחכה ליום שבוא פשוט נלמד ערבית כדי לתקשר אחד עם השני.

המלצת שמיעה: סוף סוף הספקתי להקשיב להסכת אוסלו של ד״ר @arnondeg וזה פשוט מצוין. הפודקאסט מספר מחדש את הסיפור של אוסלו מנקודת מבט חדשה. כהיסטוריונית, במיוחד אהבתי את הפרק הראשון שמדבר על החשיבות של סיפורים ואיך בוחרים נקודת התחלה.
Excited to update that I graduated with an MPhil in Modern European History from @UniofOxford. Thank you @rhodes_trust for making this possible. https://t.co/aZ01276o3L
I’m so lucky to have made such great friends at Oxford. Definitely the highlight of my time there.

Finally had my belated 2020 graduation! Feeling grateful for my family, friends and mentors who made it possible for me to attend this incredible university. Thank you to those who made my time here so meaningful. Go @StanfordHistory! https://t.co/chWdIgdH3N
למה אוקספורד כל כך יפה? https://t.co/KZTeiSyz7E
היה כיף ברלין https://t.co/4QSoTrSJbO

Was thrilled to speak on the Haaretz Weekly podcast about Israelis fighting in Ukraine. Based on an article I co-wrote with @milanczerny on the same topic for @haaretzcom. Thanks for the invitation @amirtibon. https://t.co/nsg3jsPcw5

Both Russia and Ukraine find value in a similar model - Israel, as the classic example of a modern Sparta, a lone ranger state that relies on the force of arms to survive. But this model is a double-edged sword, esp. for Israel. My latest with @milanczerny https://t.co/P7JLvvwCGy

Very interesting piece by @robkhenderson. Why higher education’s emphasis on victimhood actually gives affluent candidates an advantage. Something that I have been thinking about a lot lately. https://t.co/4ywx4JFTpd
New piece with @milanczerny for @haaretzcom about what a pop song scandal can tells us about “Israeliness” today. https://t.co/fYau4lsciM

Was great to talk to @HebrewU students today about labor zionism as part of their intro to political science class on Marxism. We also talked about applying to study abroad for postgraduate studies. https://t.co/0yTtsFQTRD

A lot has changed since the last time I was home in Israel 8 months ago: Raanana, my sleepy hometown, is now home to the new sitting PM of Israel (with much added security around his block) and is also the center of anti-covid/anti-government protests. Who would have thought?

kurdistanMentions.text: Reports that the Israeli military withdrew from the Yarmouk Basin in Syria, an area that was resisting Israeli military presence and efforts to disarm the population

assessment.summary: The captured X archive has 1 Kurdistan Lens tweet hit for Anat Peled. The social-media posture reads as kurdistan-adjacent regional signal because the strongest matched frame is Iraq, Syria, PKK/YPG, or regional language rather than explicit KRG language. Top matched terms: Syria / Rojava.
assessment.whatItWasLike: When this account mentioned the Kurdish file, the language most often clustered around Iraq, Syria, PKK/YPG, or regional language rather than explicit KRG language. I count 0 direct Kurdistan/Kurdish-region term hits, 0 security-frame hits, 0 supportive or diplomatic hits, 0 humanitarian/minority hits, and 0 energy/economic hits in the exported profile window. The latest matched tweet is dated 2024-12-28T22:12:14Z and should be opened before treating this as a final stance.
assessment.dominantFrame: Iraq, Syria, PKK/YPG, or regional language rather than explicit KRG language

Been thinking a lot lately about how the Israeli education system (which I am a product of) erases the country’s founding story which is leftist. The remnants still exist, but we no longer know how to see them. And yet, people still walk around among us who embody this past.
